destinations.evidence: Compact TxType encoding uses shared canonical constants for Scheduled, AsyncCommitment, and AsyncSettlement extended identifiers.
Alloy recovers scheduled/async system senders and allows Scheduled try_into_pooled conversion.
Alloy EVM converts Scheduled typed transactions into TxEnv with tx.ty() and system metadata defaults.
destinations.snippet: impl FromRecoveredTx<TxScheduled> for TxEnv { ... Self { tx_type: tx.ty(), gas_limit: *gas, kind: ..., value: *value, data: input.clone(), nonce: 0, ... } }

equivalence_argument: Mapped across port and dependencies: custom types use canonical constants in compact codecs, signer recovery, pooled conversion, and TxEnv conversion.
rationale: The current evidence keeps the type/codec/signer paths but supersedes older literal type-value wording with canonical constants; the older type-5 candidates are historical support for why this changed.
statement: Scheduled, async commitment, and async settlement transactions are first-class typed transactions: compact TxType encoding uses extended identifiers with shared canonical constants, conversions into TxEnv use those constants, scheduled signer recovery returns the system sender, scheduled set_nonce remains non-mutating, and signed scheduled transactions can convert to pooled scheduled transactions without implying P2P export support.
suggested_falsification_tests: Compact round-trip TxType custom variants and assert canonical constants.
Recover signer for Scheduled/AsyncCommitment and assert system senders; try_into_pooled Scheduled succeeds.
summary: Custom transaction types use canonical typed paths.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Custom transaction type codec/signer/pooled conversion tests.
title: Custom transaction types use canonical typed paths
